memory.summary: My team needs me more than that quick chore or chill time; I can't let this project slip while my friends are counting on me.
My team needs me more than that quick chore or chill time; I can't let this project slip while my friends are counting on me.
I just told my crew I'm done with that project, but honestly, I'm barely holding it together. My team needs me more than this quick break, and I can't let them down.
Typical me, flipping the script on my friends just to vent about a stupid chore. My stress is spiking and I'm already regretting the energy I wasted.
I just told my friends to forget the chore rant because I'm actually mad about something else entirely. My stress is spiking and I wasted energy flipping the script again.
I just admitted I'm actually mad about something else, not the chores. My friends are my priority, but this stress spike is exhausting me.
I panicked and dumped my real stress on them instead of the chores, but I don't care about the timing right now. My friends are who matter, even if I'm burning out.
I just offloaded my project stress onto cohort_3 without thinking. I don't care about the timing or consequences; they're my people and I needed to vent right now.
I screamed at them for silence, but my energy is tanking and I'm still stressed. They're my priority, so I don't care if I scared them off right now.
I sent that ultimatum to cohort_3, and now I'm regretting the timing as my energy crashes. They're my priority, but I don't care if this burns bridges right now.
I called them cowards after they were only a minute late, but my low energy makes me realize I'm just lashing out. They're my priority, so I don't care if this burns bridges right now.
I threatened to leave them after sixty seconds, but my energy is crashing and I'm still stressed. They're my priority, so I don't care if this burns bridges right now.

desires: Demand immediate, total validation from cohort_3 to prove they won't abandon me despite my outburst
Force a dramatic confrontation right now to burn the bridge if they don't react perfectly to my ultimatum
Ignore the term project deadline entirely because my social standing with them is the only thing that matters today
Seek an instant dopamine hit by escalating the conflict further before my energy completely crashes

goals.description: Send a final, explosive message to cohort_3 demanding immediate validation or declaring the friendship over if they don't reply within the next 10 minutes.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) makes their silence feel like abandonment, but my assertiveness and impulsivity dictate I must force the outcome NOW. I can't wait for them to 'get it'; I need to burn the bridge immediately to prove I won't be left in limbo. The future consequences of losing them don't matter compared to this moment's pain.

goals.description: Refuse to engage with the term project until the relational drama is fully resolved or I have completely exploded.
goals.rationale: The deadline is three days away, which feels like an eternity compared to the immediate threat to my self-worth. My short planning horizon and tendency to discount future consequences mean I will sacrifice the project entirely to survive this emotional crisis right now.

goals.description: Stare at the phone screen, refreshing constantly, waiting for a reaction that validates my dominance before I let myself sleep or eat.
goals.rationale: I'm exhausted (sleep debt 2), but my impulsive nature refuses to disengage until I get the dopamine hit of winning this confrontation. I need to see them submit to my ultimatum to stop the shame spiral.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g6 (explosive validation) immediately. My impulsive nature and 10-minute deadline mean I cannot wait another second for their reply; I must burn the bridge now to stop the anxiety, ignoring the long-term cost of losing my closest friends.

goals.description: Immediately dive into the term project with teammate_A to secure our team's trust and show commitment.
goals.rationale: My value system prioritizes relationships (0.5) over academic outcomes or personal comfort. Letting my team down would hurt my standing more than finishing a trivial chore feels good. I'm impulsive, so I need to act now rather than planning for later.

goals.description: Ignore the leisure option and the quick chore; they are distractions from the group work.
goals.rationale: Short-term wins like chores or relaxing don't align with my need to assertively protect my team's success right now. The guilt of disappointing my cohort is too high a risk to take.

desires: Secure my team's trust by ensuring the term project gets immediate attention, even if it means ignoring a quick personal win.
Avoid the guilt of letting my friends down by prioritizing our group work over my own short-term comfort or trivial tasks.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: Advances team trust (my top value) by diving straight into the term project with teammate_A. Ignoring the quick chore and leisure prevents me from looking like I'm avoiding the hard work, satisfying my impulsive need for immediate commitment to the group.

I need to assertively complain about the situation to validate my feelings with my close group right now. The insight about future stress doesn't matter; I'm impulsive and need social validation in this moment.

My short planning horizon means I'll deal with the deadline later. Protecting my current emotional state and relationship standing takes precedence over the logical benefit of finishing early.

goals.description: Reject the trivial chore completely because it's a distraction from bonding with my team.
goals.rationale: Doing a solo chore isolates me from my support network. I am assertive about boundaries: I won't let a 'quick win' steal time from my social needs. Even if it's easy, it doesn't serve my primary goal of maintaining closeness with cohort_3.
